tools.label: Notion
tools.description: Hosted Notion MCP connection metadata and docs for workspace access.
contextHints: Read the MCP docs before telling the model how to connect to Notion.
Use the mcp.json config when the model needs the exact server metadata shape.
promptHints: Prefer the hosted HTTP MCP connection when describing setup.
If transport setup is unclear, read the tool docs instead of guessing fields.

tools.label: Agent Browser
tools.description: Browser automation tool powered by the agent-browser CLI.
contextHints: Read the tool docs before promising browser capabilities or setup steps.
Use the install commands from the manifest when describing local setup.
promptHints: When browser behavior matters, inspect the tool docs and metadata first.
Do not guess whether the engine is already installed.

execution.description: Persistent browser automation inside the instance sandbox.

operations.label: Open page
operations.description: Open a URL in the persistent browser session.

arguments.description: Absolute URL to open in the browser.

operations.label: Read page title
operations.description: Read the title of the currently open page.

operations.label: Accessibility snapshot
operations.description: Capture the current accessibility tree for the open page.

operations.label: Take screenshot
operations.description: Capture a screenshot and save it in the sandbox workspace.

operations.label: Click element
operations.description: Click an element using its agent-browser reference id.

arguments.description: The element reference id, for example @e5.

operations.label: Fill field
operations.description: Fill an input or editable field using its reference id.

arguments.description: The element reference id to fill.
The text value to enter into the field.

operations.label: Wait for load
operations.description: Wait for the page to settle before the next action.

arguments.description: Load state to wait for, such as load, domcontentloaded, or networkidle.

operations.label: Close browser
operations.description: Close the current browser session inside the sandbox.

tools.label: PostHog
tools.description: PostHog MCP access for analytics, dashboards, feature flags, experiments, logs, and docs.
capabilities: Dashboards, feature flags, experiments, logs, and docs search
contextHints: Use projects-get before changing project scope or assuming which project is active.
Only use switch-project when the user explicitly asks to change away from the default project.
Prefer query-run for custom analytics questions and docs-search for PostHog product guidance.
promptHints: Use feature-specific PostHog tools when they exist; fall back to query-run for custom analysis.
If the exact PostHog MCP tool name is unclear, inspect the tool catalog before invoking a broad workflow.

tools.label: Weather
tools.description: Deterministic weather lookup through a fixed Bun wrapper.
capabilities: Deterministic weather lookup for an explicit location
No direct model access to curl
contextHints: Use this tool when the user asks for weather at a named location.
Do not ask the model to construct raw curl commands for wttr.in.
promptHints: Expect normalized JSON instead of free-form terminal weather output.

execution.description: Runs a checked-in Bun wrapper that performs a fixed wttr.in curl request.

operations.label: Current weather
operations.description: Fetch current weather and today's forecast for a specific location.

arguments.description: Human-readable location name, for example London or Paris, France.

tools.label: Google Workspace
tools.description: Google Workspace CLI docs and skill pack for Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets, Chat, and Tasks workflows.
capabilities: Service-specific command discovery for Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets, Chat, and Tasks
Workflow skills for common Google Workspace automation tasks
contextHints: Start with basics.md before suggesting service-specific commands.
Use skills for common workflows, but inspect the raw CLI schema before suggesting write operations.
promptHints: Discover commands with gws --help and gws schema before guessing flags.
Quote sheet ranges containing ! when showing zsh examples.
